free_text: Excitation functions for the formation of various
gadolinium and lutetium isotopes by (d,xn) reactions
Eu-153 and Y-nat target data given. Same as thesis.
'A Study of the Chemical Effects of the Electron
Capture Decay Process by means of Moessbauer
Each foil was made by evaporating the appropriate
oxide, equivalent to 10 ug/cm2 of the metal
(300 ug/cm2 of the metal oxide), on to a pure Al foil
Standardised with Ta-182, Ag-110m and Ir-192
Al foils of various thicknesses were placed
between the target foils to reduce the energy of the
deuteron beam (nominal beam energy of 30 MeV).
The mean bombarding energy for each foil was
calculated by means of a computer program based on
the range-energy relationships given in the
Beam current measured by a Faraday cup.
The oxide dissolved in 1M hydrochloric acid.
Inactive carrier solution was then added, followed by
oxalic acid to precipitate lanthanide oxalates.
The oxalate precipitate was centrifuged down and the
supernate discarded. 1 ml of 12M hydrochloric acid
was added to dissolve the oxalate.
The samples were first counted within 2 hours
of the end of the deuteron irradiation.
Weighted means of activities from the areas were taken
Overall coefficient of variation for the cross
SD: DECAY-DATA was corrected in Subent 006.
Dysprosium was used as a carrier.
